title: The people behind the papers - Ksenia Kudryashova and Olga Kyrchanova.
abstract: Hox genes, such as Drosophila Abdominal-B (Abd-B), regulate the morphogenesis of specific anatomical regions along the anteroposterior axis in vertebrates. In their work, Olga Kyrchanova and colleagues find that specific initiator sequences control the activity states of the regulatory domains that direct Abd-B expression in the designated segments during Drosophila development. To know more about their work, we spoke to Ksenia Kudryashova and the corresponding author, Olga Kyrchanova, Group Leader, Institute of Gene Biology, Russian Academy of Sciences, Russia.
